digest.component_or_area: release and store-compatibility surfaces (release.yml + store format-version gate)
propositions: release.yml publishes on any human-pushed v* tag with no proof the tagged SHA is reviewed-and-landed main, so releases must instead be initiated from protected main via workflow_dispatch with version-lockstep assertion and OIDC trusted-publishing.

There is no committed store-format-version/required-capability record, so a v1.0 binary must ship a compatibility gate that fails closed on present-but-incompatible or corrupt records across all store-mutation/publish paths.

The compatibility gate must treat an absent record (every pre-v1.0 store) as legacy version 0 / compatible / fail-open, auto-writing the record on the next covered write, and remain rollback-safe for older binaries.

python -m build bakes COMMIT=None into the published wheel and CI installs the source tree rather than the artifact, so the exact hash-verified wheel/sdist must be tested and published with the correct non-null source commit.

The release pipeline resolves actions, build tools, and a privileged OIDC-context publisher from mutable references, so every executable must be pinned by immutable SHA/digest and checkouts must not persist credentials.

Removed env vars are silently ignored and removed TOML keys get only a generic typo warning, so removed inputs affecting store location, write/sync gates, auth, or lifecycle policy must fail loud with a targeted migration message and nonzero exit.
